description: FeedList.svelte renders each visible row by mounting FeedRow with literal placeholders body="", createdAt={0}, updatedAt={0}, tags={[]}. There is no projection of Note.frontmatter (createdAt, updatedAt, tags) nor Note.body into the row. As wired, every produced row will display 'Created: 1970-01-01 09:00', empty preview, and zero tag chips at runtime — directly violating REQ-FEED-001 (createdAt/updatedAt timestamps), REQ-FEED-002 (2-line body preview), REQ-FEED-003 (tag pill rendering), and REQ-FEED-017 (post-save updatedAt refresh has nothing to refresh). The contract scope explicitly enumerates FeedList.svelte as part of Sprint 1 deliverable and 'Full working feed list with row click ... and all purity/IPC boundary constraints enforced' (contracts/sprint-1.md:67), so deferring this to a downstream feature is not legal under the approved contract. The integration tests pass because they assert only row count and the missing data, but they do not assert any timestamp / preview / tag content.
recommendation: Either (a) extend FeedDomainSnapshot to carry per-noteId metadata (frontmatter + body) and pass real values in FeedList, or (b) add a feature-scoped projection store that resolves noteId -> { body, createdAt, updatedAt, tags } before render. Then add DOM assertions in FeedList.dom.vitest.ts that verify [data-testid=row-created-at], [data-testid=row-body-preview], and [data-testid=tag-chip] reflect the snapshot data, not zero/empty placeholders.
